twitter_thread: 70% of open source projects die within 6 months. Only 3% make it past 2 years.
Chatto open sourced with just 10k lines of code, proving less can be more
EVE Online's Carbon engine open source move cut their dev costs by 40% annually
Open sourcing without a maintainer team is a recipe for disaster: 90% of issues go unfixed
Fenris Creations built a 10,000-strong community by open sourcing EVE's engine
GitLab and GitHub can save open source projects up to 30% on infrastructure costs
What's the most surprising benefit you've seen from open sourcing a software project? #oss #softwaredevelopment
pack.linkedin: 70% of open source projects fail within 6 months, but 3% thrive past 2 years. Chatto's early open source move with just 10k lines of code and EVE Online's Carbon engine, which cut dev costs by 40%, reveal key tactics. Without a clear maintainer team, 90% of issues go unfixed. Building a community, like Fenris Creations' 10,000-strong one, is crucial. Tools like GitLab and GitHub can save up to 30% on infrastructure. What surprising benefits have you seen from open sourcing a software project?
